name: Cosmological upper limit on the sum of neutrino masses
requirement: Any candidate framework must predict the sum of neutrino masses Sum m_nu below the cosmological upper limit. Combined CMB + BAO + supernova analyses give Sum m_nu < 0.072 eV (DESI BAO Y1 + Planck + Pantheon+, 95% CL), an order of magnitude below the laboratory KATRIN bound on the lightest neutrino mass.

regime.description: Cosmological scales; neutrino masses suppress structure growth at scales smaller than the neutrino free-streaming length.

current_bound.units: eV (95% CL upper limit on Sum m_nu)
current_bound.experiment: DESI BAO Y1 + CMB + SN Ia
current_bound.reference: DESI Collaboration, 'DESI 2024 VI: Cosmological Constraints from the Measurements of Baryon Acoustic Oscillations', arXiv:2404.03002 (2024)

what_it_excludes: Tightens already-stringent bounds. Together with neutrino-oscillation Delta m_31^2 ~ 2.5 x 10^-3 eV^2 and Delta m_21^2 ~ 7.5 x 10^-5 eV^2, the cosmological bound disfavors the inverted-hierarchy mass ordering at ~2 sigma. Constrains BSM scenarios with extra relativistic neutrinos or non-standard neutrino properties.

parameterization.name: Standard cosmological perturbation theory with massive neutrinos as a free-streaming hot dark matter component. Effect parameterized by Sum m_nu and the number of mass eigenstates.
parameterization.reference: Lesgourgues and Pastor, Phys. Rep. 429, 307 (2006); Lattanzi and Gerbino, Frontiers in Physics 5, 70 (2018).
parameterization.summary: Massive neutrinos suppress matter power spectrum P(k) at k > k_FS by a factor (1 - 8 f_nu) where f_nu = Omega_nu / Omega_m.

procedural.predicted_observable: CMB lensing power, BAO scale, and matter power spectrum from galaxy surveys, all sensitive to Sum m_nu in calculable ways.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: Sum m_nu < 0.072 eV (DESI Y1 + CMB + SN, 95% CL); < 0.12 eV (Planck + BAO 2018); future Euclid + Roman + DESI Y5 + LiteBIRD aim at 0.02 eV sensitivity.
framework_inputs: If the framework predicts non-standard neutrino properties (sterile, decaying, secret-interaction), implement them in CLASS/CAMB.
Sum m_nu is read directly from the framework's neutrino mass spectrum.

primary_references.citation: DESI Collaboration, 'DESI 2024 VI: Cosmological Constraints from the Measurements of Baryon Acoustic Oscillations'
Planck Collaboration, 'Planck 2018 results. VI. Cosmological parameters', A&A 641, A6 (2020)
Lesgourgues and Pastor, 'Massive neutrinos and cosmology', Phys. Rep. 429, 307 (2006)
